Everyone here is predicting that the killer will be motivated by a loneliness induced  hatred of couples, which is fairly reasonable. But what if the twist is  the complete opposite: that there’s actually two killers, they’re a couple, and their idea of a double date is killing other couples.
































Edit: Thanks for all the likes! I have now seen the movie. I loved it! I think there are some pacing issues in the drive-in theater scene, and the climax has some obnoxious exposition and confusing circumstances surrounding a bullet wound, but overall, the film was consistently thrilling, funny, and sweet throughout. 

The opening scene is incredibly strong and establishes the tone, visual style, and humor of the film very well. I loved the cinematography throughout: I enjoyed its color palette, and something about the film’s compositions really appeals to me. 
 
I found the characters likable and very well performed. I loved how they fought the killer together, it was a great way to marry the slasher and romance genres, transforming the act of fighting a serial killer into a method by which the couple could bond. 

I have mixed thoughts on the killer’s costume. I love the heart eyes, and in the right lighting (as seen in the poster, which notably enshrouds the entirety of their lower body in darkness), the costume looks great. But in some ways, it’s generic. I do like the silhouette and movement of their coat, but its pure black color scheme is kind of uncreative and clashes with the mask, which I think is meant to be evocative of a cherub, and doesn’t really have anything to do with the rest of the costume. 

Perhaps they could have added something evocative of wings to the coat to bridge the head to the rest of the body. I also have mixed thoughts on the mask: I can’t decide whether I like or dislike its veiny texture. On another note, the killer’s arsenal is great and fits their motif very well.

Overall, I had great time, and of course, it was great to see my prediction was somewhat correct! I give it a 7-8/10, or a 75-80/100. My thoughts on some of the film’s aspects are not entirely set in stone, but I’m fairly positive about these scores. I recommend it if you can handle gore and are in the mood for a likable comedy-horror film.
